ud1.10:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
ud1.10:1.3: Now at that time Bāhiya Dārucīriya was living at Suppāraka on the seashore, honored, respected, esteemed, venerated, and revered, a recipient of robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick.
ud1.10:1.4: Then, while Bāhiya Dārucīriya had gone into seclusion and was alone in retreat, this thought arose in his mind:
ud1.10:1.5: “Whoever in the world are *arahants* or have entered the path to arahantship, I am one of them.”
ud1.10:10.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having instructed Bāhiya Dārucīriya with this brief instruction, departed.
ud1.10:10.2: Then, not long after the *Bhagavā* had gone, a cow with a young calf charged Bāhiya Dārucīriya and killed him.
ud1.10:11.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, after walking for alms in Sāvatthī, after the meal, returning from the almsround, together with several *bhikkhus* left the city and saw Bāhiya Dārucīriya dead;
ud1.10:11.3: “Take Bāhiya Dārucīriya’s body, *bhikkhus*; placing it on a bier, carry it out and cremate it; and build a stupa for him.
ud1.10:11.4: Your companion in the holy life, *bhikkhus*, has died.”
ud1.10:12.1: “Yes, bhante,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the *Bhagavā*. Then, taking Bāhiya Dārucīriya’s body, placing it on a bier, carrying it out, cremating it, and building a stupa for him, they approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side.
ud1.10:12.2: Seated to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:12.3: “Bāhiya Dārucīriya’s body has been burned, bhante, and a stupa has been built for him.
ud1.10:12.4: What is his destination, what is his fate beyond?”
ud1.10:12.5: “Bāhiya Dārucīriya was wise, *bhikkhus*; he practiced in accordance with *dhamma* [*dhammassānudhammaṁ*], and he did not trouble me with a matter concerning *dhamma* [*dhammādhikaraṇaṁ*].
ud1.10:13.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, understanding the meaning of this, at that time expressed this inspired utterance:
ud1.10:14.2: fire and wind do not gain a footing;
ud1.10:14.4: the sun does not give light;
ud1.10:14.5: there the moon does not glow,
ud1.10:15.1: And when he has known it for himself,
ud1.10:15.2: the sage, the brahmin through silence;
ud1.10:15.3: then from form and the formless,
ud1.10:15.4: from pleasure and pain he is freed.”
ud1.10:16.1: This too is an inspired utterance spoken by the *Bhagavā*; thus I heard.
ud1.10:17.1: Three on awakening and the Hum-Hum,
ud1.10:17.3: the goat, battle, the matted-hair ascetics,
ud1.10:17.4: and Bāhiya: these are the ten.
ud1.10:2.1: Then a *devatā* who had formerly been a blood-relative of Bāhiya Dārucīriya, compassionate and wanting his welfare, knew with her own mind the thought in Bāhiya Dārucīriya’s mind and approached him. Having approached, she said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:2.2: “You, Bāhiya, are neither an *arahant*, nor have you entered the path to arahantship.
ud1.10:2.3: And you do not have that practice by which you might be an *arahant* or might have entered the path to arahantship.”
ud1.10:3.1: “Then who, in the world with its gods, are *arahants* or have entered the path to arahantship?”
ud1.10:3.2: “There is, Bāhiya, in the northern lands, a city named Sāvatthī.
ud1.10:3.3: There that *Bhagavā* is now dwelling, an *arahant*, a perfectly awakened one.
ud1.10:3.4: For that *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya, is indeed an *arahant* and teaches *dhamma* for arahantship.”
ud1.10:4.1: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya, stirred by that *devatā*, immediately departed from Suppāraka.
ud1.10:4.2: Staying just one night in each place, he went to Sāvatthī, to Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
ud1.10:4.3: Now at that time several *bhikkhus* were walking in the open air.
ud1.10:4.4: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya approached those *bhikkhus*. Having approached, he said this to them:
ud1.10:4.5: “Where now, bhante, is the *Bhagavā* dwelling, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one?
ud1.10:4.6: We wish to see that *Bhagavā*, that *arahant*, the perfectly awakened one.”
ud1.10:4.7: “The *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya, has entered among the houses for alms.”
ud1.10:5.1: Then Bāhiya Dārucīriya, hurrying, left Jeta’s Grove, entered Sāvatthī, and saw the *Bhagavā* walking for alms in Sāvatthī—gracious, inspiring confidence, with tranquil *indriyas* [*santindriyaṁ*], with a tranquil mind, having attained the supreme peace of taming, tamed, guarded, with restrained *indriyas* [*yatindriyaṁ*], a great one.
ud1.10:5.2: Seeing him, he approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he fell with his head at the *Bhagavā*’s feet and said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:5.3: “Let the *Bhagavā*, bhante, teach me *dhamma*; let the Well-Gone One teach me *dhamma*,
ud1.10:5.4: that would be for my welfare and happiness for a long time.”
ud1.10:5.5: When this was said, the *Bhagavā* said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:5.6: “This is not the right time yet, Bāhiya; we have entered among the houses for alms.”
ud1.10:6.1: A second time Bāhiya Dārucīriya said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:6.2: “But, bhante, it is hard to know whether there may be danger to the *Bhagavā*’s life or to my life.
ud1.10:6.4: let the Well-Gone One teach me *dhamma*, that would be for my welfare and happiness for a long time.”
ud1.10:6.5: A second time the *Bhagavā* said this to Bāhiya Dārucīriya:
ud1.10:6.6: “This is not the right time yet, Bāhiya; we have entered among the houses for alms.”
ud1.10:7.1: A third time Bāhiya Dārucīriya said this to the *Bhagavā*:
ud1.10:7.2: “But, bhante, it is hard to know whether there may be danger to the *Bhagavā*’s life or to my life.
ud1.10:7.4: let the Well-Gone One teach me *dhamma*, that would be for my welfare and happiness for a long time.”
ud1.10:8.1: “Therefore, Bāhiya, this is how you should train:
ud1.10:8.10: Just this is the end of *dukkha*.”
ud1.10:8.2: ‘In the seen there will be just the seen, in the heard there will be just the heard, in the sensed there will be just the sensed, in the cognized there will be just the cognized.’
ud1.10:8.3: That is how you should train, Bāhiya.
ud1.10:8.4: For when, Bāhiya, in the seen there will be just the seen, in the heard there will be just the heard, in the sensed there will be just the sensed, in the cognized there will be just the cognized,
ud1.10:8.5: then, Bāhiya, you will not be with that.
ud1.10:8.6: When, Bāhiya, you are not with that,
ud1.10:8.7: then, Bāhiya, you will not be there.
ud1.10:8.8: When, Bāhiya, you are not there,
ud1.10:8.9: then, Bāhiya, you will be neither here nor beyond nor in between the two.
ud1.10:9.1: Then, through this brief teaching of *dhamma* [*dhammadesanāya*] from the *Bhagavā*, Bāhiya Dārucīriya’s mind [*citta*] was immediately liberated from the *āsavas* through non-clinging [*anupādāya*].
